info.name: Flagify API
info.description: Postman collection for the full Flagify admin, runtime, analytics, import/export, and snapshot API surface.

item.name: Projects
item.description: Project bootstrap and top-level tenancy. Creating a project also creates the default environments: development, staging, and production.

request.description: Create a new project. This is the root container for environments, clients, segments, flags, and keys.
List visible projects. Root can see all projects; scoped keys only see their bound project.
Get one project by id.
Update project metadata.
Soft delete a project.

item.name: Keys
item.description: API keys control access. Project admin keys manage project resources, project read keys inspect them, and client runtime keys resolve runtime config for one client.

request.description: Create a project admin key for managing flags, environments, segments, clients, overrides, and additional keys inside the project.
Create a read-only project key. Use this for inspection flows such as listing resources, reading runtime config for any client, and downloading snapshots.
Create a runtime key bound to a single client. This key resolves runtime config without exposing broader project access.
List keys, optionally filtered by project.
Get one key by id.
Revoke a key.

item.name: Environments
item.description: Environments let the same flag behave differently across release contexts such as development, staging, production, or a custom QA environment.

request.description: List the environments in the project. New projects start with development, staging, and production automatically.
Create an additional environment. Environments are where environment-specific flag rules and defaults live.
Get one environment by id.
Update environment metadata and change-request protection.
Mark production as requiring change requests.
Delete an environment.

item.name: Segments
item.description: Segments are reusable audiences defined by client attributes. A segment can be referenced by many flag rules to avoid duplicating targeting logic.

request.description: Create a reusable audience for premium customers. This example matches clients whose metadata.plan is premium.
List all active segments available for use in environment-specific flag rules.
Get one segment by id.
Update segment rules.
Delete a segment.

item.name: Clients
item.description: Clients are the runtime subjects being evaluated. Their metadata is what rule conditions and segments target.

request.description: Create a client with metadata that can be used for targeting, segment membership, and sticky rollout bucketing.
List clients.
Get one client by id.
Update client metadata or status.
Soft delete a client.
Create or replace a client-specific flag override.
List overrides for one client.
Delete a client override.

item.name: Flags
item.description: Flags define the feature itself. Environment-specific config adds ordered rules, rollout percentage, schedules, and served variants or values.

request.description: Create a multivariate flag with named variants and payloads. This example models an experiment-style rollout between control and variant_a.
Get environment-specific flag config.
Set production-specific rules for the flag. This example serves variant_a to 50% of the premium_users segment using sticky bucketing on client key.
List project flags and inspect their top-level definition, including kind, variants, prerequisites, and stale status.
Get one flag by id.
Update a flag definition.
Reset environment config to inherited defaults.
Delete or archive a flag.

item.name: Runtime
item.description: Runtime endpoints evaluate flags for a client in a chosen environment. They also expose a snapshot format for local or offline evaluation consumers.

request.description: Resolve config for the client bound to the runtime key. The environment query parameter selects which environment rules to apply.
Resolve one flag for the client bound to the runtime key.
Resolve config for any client in a specific environment using a project read or admin key. Useful for debugging targeting decisions.

item.name: Resolve Project Client Config With Query Environment

request.description: Resolve config for any project client using the environment query parameter.
Resolve one flag for a project client in an explicit environment.

item.name: Resolve Project Client Flag With Query Environment

request.description: Resolve one flag for a project client using the environment query parameter.
Download an environment snapshot containing flag definitions, segments, and rules. This is the contract intended for local evaluation consumers or future SDKs.
Ingest batched evaluation events from local evaluators or edge services.

item.name: Events
item.description: Evaluation events are the raw exposure trail. They show what value was served, why it was served, which rule matched, and the evaluation context.

request.description: List stored evaluation events for debugging and analysis. Filter by environment, flag, or client as needed.

item.name: Identities
item.description: Identity APIs create first-class runtime subjects and manage persisted traits independently from legacy clients.

request.description: Create an identity with initial traits.
List identities.
Get one identity by id.
Update identity metadata or status.
Soft delete an identity.
List persisted traits for one identity.
Bulk set and unset identity traits.
Upsert a single identity trait.
Evaluate all or selected flags for an identity with transient traits.
Resolve config for a specific identity.
Resolve one flag for a specific identity.

item.name: Operations
item.description: Operational APIs cover audit logs, analytics, change requests, import/export, code references, and stale-flag reporting.

request.description: List audit logs with optional filters.
Aggregate evaluations by flag.
Aggregate evaluations by variant.
Aggregate evaluations by environment.
List recent evaluation activity.
Create a change request for protected flag config changes.
List change requests.
Get one change request by id.
Approve or reject a pending change request.
Apply an approved change request.
Export deterministic project configuration.
Preview an import plan without applying changes.
Apply an import plan and replace conflicting resources.
Ingest code references for flags.
List ingested code references.
Delete one code reference.
Report likely stale flags.
Re-fetch a snapshot using If-None-Match.
